title: erythronium hendersonii
intro: Erythronium hendersonii, also known as the Wallflower Trout Lily, is a species of flowering plant in the lily family, characterized by its nodding, bell-shaped flowers and strap-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Erythronium hendersonii include its ability to reduce swelling and promote wound healing, making it a popular remedy for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by drying the roots and leaves, then infusing them in hot water to create a tea that can be applied topically or taken internally.
